consequence.description: Market orders at mid-price or with random slippage create unrealistic fills that overestimate execution quality; passive limit orders treated as aggressive creates false liquidity assumptions, causing backtest returns to exceed what live trading can achieve

evidence_refs.summary: a3dc039 Derived from BD: BD-026

core.when: When implementing TWAP scheduling or TimeTableGenerator interval validation
core.action: Maintain consistent 3-second minimum interval constraint across both TWAP algorithm (BD-005) and TimeTableGenerator (BD-015); when changing the interval threshold in one layer, must update the other layer to preserve consistency

consequence.description: Inconsistent interval constraints between TWAP and TimeTableGenerator cause confusing duplicate error messages and create coupling risk where changes to one layer without the other lead to unpredictable validation failures

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-051

core.when: When configuring TqMultiAccount with multiple trading accounts
core.action: Register accounts in reverse order (last account first) — the chain traversal semantics require reverse ordering for correct message routing to each account

consequence.description: Forward ordering causes orders and messages to be routed to the wrong account due to chain traversal semantics; this silent routing error leads to positions being opened in incorrect accounts and potential unintended trading in wrong accounts

evidence_refs.summary: a3dc039 Derived from BD: BD-037

core.when: When implementing strategies using wait_update with computational delays between calls
core.action: Account for simulation timing accuracy vs speed trade-off: simulation time only advances when wait_update is called with new quotes; strategies with long computational delays may have simulation time that does not advance proportionally to real time

consequence.description: Strategies with long computational delays between wait_update calls will have simulation time lagging behind real time, causing execution timing mismatches that make backtest results unreproducible in live trading

evidence_refs.summary: a3dc039 Derived from BD: BD-056

core.when: When implementing or refactoring the AR momentum strategy thresholds
core.action: Use the fixed AR bands: long entries when 110 < AR < 150 (moderate overheating), short entries when 50 < AR < 90 (moderate weakness), with symmetric exit thresholds around neutral. Do not use symmetric ranges like 90-110 for both entries

consequence.description: Changing AR thresholds to symmetric ranges or arbitrary values removes the asymmetric entry logic designed to exploit the empirical observation that markets sustain overheating longer than weakness, potentially causing strategy to enter positions at suboptimal timing

evidence_refs.summary: a3dc039 Derived from BD: BD-013

validation_threshold: ar_entry_long not in (110, 150) OR ar_entry_short not in (50, 90) → WARN

core.when: When implementing order execution logic in backtesting
core.action: Execute sell orders before buy orders when both are pending — verify no reliance on leverage or intraday credit

consequence.description: Buying before selling creates hidden leverage when capital is insufficient; in live trading, buy orders may be rejected due to insufficient funds, causing backtest-live inconsistency

evidence_refs.summary: a3dc039 Derived from BD: BD-013

core.when: When implementing IOC (Immediate-Or-Cancel) order handling in simulation
core.action: Cancel IOC orders immediately if fill cannot occur at the required price — implement all-or-nothing execution semantics, do not support partial fills for IOC orders

consequence.description: IOC orders with partial fill support violate exchange IOC behavior semantics; strategies expecting immediate cancellation will receive unintended fills at worse prices, causing backtest-live execution mismatch

evidence_refs.summary: a3dc039 Derived from BD: BD-032

core.when: When implementing custom risk rule subclasses extending TqRiskRule
core.action: Override _could_insert_order as a pre-check hook (called before order submission) and _on_insert_order as a post-check hook (called after order submission) — both hooks must be called within TqApi insert_order flow to maintain correct risk management sequencing

consequence.description: Overriding risk check hooks incorrectly or outside the TqApi insert_order flow breaks the template method pattern, causing pre-checks to run after order submission or post-checks to never execute, resulting in uncaught risk violations

evidence_refs.summary: a3dc039 Derived from BD: BD-043

validation_threshold: custom_risk_rule_hooks not called in insert_order_flow → WARN

core.when: When implementing trading operations in multi-account configuration
core.action: Pass explicit account parameter to every trading operation (insert_order, get_position, get_account, etc.) when multiple accounts are active — do not rely on implicit default account routing

consequence.description: Omitting explicit account parameter routes orders to the default account in multi-account mode, potentially executing trades on the wrong broker account and causing position/confirmation mismatches

evidence_refs.summary: a3dc039 Derived from BD: BD-045
